title: Course: CS 10 | EECS at UC Berkeley
content: Course: CS 10 | EECS at UC Berkeley CS 10. The Beauty and Joy of Computing Catalog Description: An introductory course for students with minimal prior exposure to computer science. Prepares students for future computer science courses and empowers them to utilize programming to solve problems in their field of study. Presents an overview of the history, great principles, and transformative applications of computer science, as well as a comprehensive introduction to programming. Topics include abstraction, recursion, algorithmic complexity, higher-order functions, concurrency, social implications of computing (privacy, education, algorithmic bias), and engaging research areas (data science, AI, HCI). Students will program in Snap! (a friendly graphical language) and Python, and will design and implement two projects of their choice. Units: 4 Related Areas: Education (EDUC) Credit Restrictions: Students will receive no credit for 10 after having taken W10, 61A, 61B, or 61C. Formats: Fall: 2.0 hours of lecture, 1.0 hours of discussion, and 4.0 hours of laboratory per week Summer: 4.0 hours of lecture, 2.0 hours of discussion, and 8.0 hours of laboratory per week Spring: 2.0 hours of lecture, 1.0 hours of discussion, and 4.0 hours of laboratory per week Grading Basis: letter Final Exam Status: Written final exam conducted during the scheduled final exam period Class Schedule (Spring 2026): CS 10 - MoWeFr 10:00-10:59, Evans 60 - Dan Garcia Class Notes - Time conflicts are allowed Links: EECS Course Archives (CalNet authentication required) CS enrollment policies EE enrollment policies HKN course maps
